step_by_step_thinking:
I agree with agent1's assessment. Klippel-Trenaunay Syndrome (KTS) indeed presents with characteristic features such as vascular malformations and venous dilatation, which can lead to pulsatile varicose veins in the lower extremity because of abnormal blood flow dynamics caused by arteriovenous shunts present in KTS patients. The presence of both venous and arterial anomalies explains why there might be palpable pulses even though it’s primarily described as a venous disorder.
